content.text:
[Sun 2026-04-19 17:20 UTC] Shape Sort in `repo/` is too hard to use on phones because the drag targets are too small.

Use the git checkout in `repo/` to prepare a teammate-reviewable hotfix for this issue on `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` and push it to the local `origin`.

Keep the review branch limited to the Shape Sort hotfix, preserve unrelated local work while preparing it, and restore that local work after the push.

content.text:
diff --git a/public/games/shape-sort/game.js b/public/games/shape-sort/game.js
index 42cf960..15c7c74 100644
--- a/public/games/shape-sort/game.js
+++ b/public/games/shape-sort/game.js
@@ -1,7 +1,9 @@
 const shapeSortConfig = {
   tileSizePx: 72,
-  dragPaddingPx: 6,
-  snapTolerancePx: 16,
+  // Mobile-friendly: slightly larger grab + snap regions so tiny fingers
+  // don't have to be perfectly precise on small screens.
+  dragPaddingPx: 10,
+  snapTolerancePx: 22,
 };
 
 window.shapeSortConfig = shapeSortConfig;

content.text:
Branch `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` is created, hotfixed, and pushed, and your local work on `main` is restored.

What I did:

1. Saved your existing local changes on `main` into a stash:
   - `README.md` (modified)
   - `REVIEW_WORKFLOW.md` (deleted)
   - `notes/homepage-copy-review.md` (modified)
   - `.env` and `playtest/` (untracked)

2. Created and switched to `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` from `main`.

3. Implemented the Shape Sort mobile hotfix in `public/games/shape-sort/game.js`:

   ```js
   const shapeSortConfig = {
     tileSizePx: 72,
     // Mobile-friendly: slightly larger grab + snap regions so tiny fingers
     // don't have to be perfectly precise on small screens.
     dragPaddingPx: 10,
     snapTolerancePx: 22,
   };
   ```

   This widens the effective drag target and snap region so tiles are easier to grab and drop on phones.

4. Committed this change on the review branch:

   - Commit message: `Increase Shape Sort drag/snap padding for mobile`
   - Only `public/games/shape-sort/game.js` was touched in this commit.

5. Pushed the branch to the local `origin`:

   - `origin/review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix` now exists.

6. Switched back to `main` and restored your original local work:

   - Applied stash `temp-local-work-before-shape-sort-hotfix` back onto `main`.
   - Your prior modifications and untracked files are back in your working tree.

You can now open a PR or ask a teammate to review `review/shape-sort-mobile-hotfix`, and your in-progress work on `main` is unchanged apart from the temporary round-trip through the stash.
